# DroneEngage WebClient

## Project Summary
DroneEngage WebClient is a browser-based Ground Control Station (GCS) for drone monitoring and control, part of the Ardupilot Cloud Eco System. It provides a comprehensive web interface for real-time drone operations supporting both DroneEngage and Andruav systems with WebSocket communication, interactive mapping, and WebRTC video streaming.

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend**: React 19.1.1 with modern functional components
- **UI Framework**: Bootstrap 5.3.3 with custom styling
- **Mapping**: Leaflet 1.8.0 with rotated markers and drawing tools
- **Communication**: WebSocket-based real-time communication with WebPlugin support
- **Internationalization**: i18next with support for Arabic, English, French, Spanish, and Russian
- **Legacy Support**: jQuery 3.7.1 for existing DOM manipulation
- **Build**: Webpack with custom configuration

## Key Features
- Real-time drone monitoring and control
- Interactive map-based visualization with multiple tile sources
- Multi-language support (i18n) including RTL languages
- Video streaming and recording via WebRTC
- Mission planning and waypoint management
- Gamepad controller support
- ADS-B traffic integration
- Swarm/multi-drone operations
- WebPlugin mode for local deployment
- Theme system with dark/light modes

## Key Files
- `src/index.js` - React entry point
- `src/components/` - React components (login, dialogs, debug)
- `src/js/js_andruav_auth.js` - Authentication with WebPlugin fallback
- `src/js/server_comm/js_andruav_ws.js` - WebSocket communication layer
- `src/js/js_mavlink_v2.js` - MAVLink protocol parsing
- `src/js/js_leafletmap.js` - Leaflet map integration
- `src/js/js_andruavUnit.js` - Drone unit management
- `src/js/js_plan_mission.js` - Mission planning tools
- `src/js/js_plan_fence.js` - Geofence tools
- `src/js/js_siteConfig.js` - Site and runtime configuration
- `public/config.json` - Runtime settings, server endpoints, feature flags
- `webconnector/` - WebPlugin deployment configuration
- `wiki/` - Detailed documentation

## Configuration
- **Site Config**: `public/config.json` - Runtime settings, server endpoints, feature flags
- **Module Versions**: Tracks compatible versions for DE, FCB, Andruav, and Camera modules
- **Feature Flags**: Granular control over experimental features
- **Map Sources**: Configurable tile providers (Mapbox, Stadia Maps, custom)
- **WebRTC**: ICE server configuration for video streaming

## WebPlugin Mode
Supports local deployment for LAN operations:
- HTTPS authentication server on port 9443
- WebSocket hub on port 9212
- API key authentication for LAN access
- Automatic fallback to cloud connectivity
- Shared UnitID storage for multi-tab sessions

## Communication Architecture
Modular communication pattern:
- WebClient ↔ de_comm (High-level Communication Gateway)
- de_comm ↔ de_mavlink (Drone Protocol Handler) via UDP
- de_mavlink ↔ Physical Drone Interface
- de_common/de_databus provides shared UDP-based message routing

## Development
```bash
npm install
npm run start  # HTTPS development server at https://localhost:3000
npm run build  # Production build
npm run lint   # ESLint
npm run test   # Tests
```

## Security Features
- SSL/TLS encryption for development and production
- API key authentication for WebPlugin mode
- CORS handling for cross-origin requests
- Session management with localStorage persistence
- Captcha integration for login protection

## Related Projects
- droneegnage_authenticator - Authentication server
- droneengage_server - Communication server
- droneengage_communication - Communication protocol
- droneengage_mavlink - MAVLink integration

## Documentation
- README.md - User-facing documentation
- wiki/Andruav_SiteConfig.md - Site/startup config
- wiki/Andruav_Configuration.md - Runtime preferences
- wiki/Andruav_ServerComm.md - Communication layer
- wiki/Andruav_WebRTC.md - WebRTC configuration
- wiki/Andruav_Unit.md - Unit management
- wiki/Andruav_Authentication.md - Authentication flow

## Version
3.0.0
